summary: Apple’s once-glorified assistant has fallen way behind flashier AI like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude . After years of stunted updates and stiff competition from Google, OpenAI, and Amazon, Siri is potentially on the brink of a reinvention . Whispers suggest this gamble could pay off and finally turn Apple stock's fortunes around .  Siri was created by Apple in 2011 and has been missing in action since then . ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Grok have evolved into sharp, context-savvy bots . Apple tried to turn things around with its massive ‘Apple Intelligence” rebrand in mid-2024, backed by savvy on-device models .  Recent reports suggest that Siri’s brain power could potentially be outsourced, marking a major U-turn for a company that has looked to build everything in-house . Apple shares popped 2% on the news, with Apple stock in the green . The shake-up follows big leadership moves, too .  The company has reportedly asked OpenAI and Anthropic to train AI models that can run on Apple’s cloud servers . That essentially means a much faster rollout and fewer AI hiccups . Samsung used Google's Gemini for its smartphones and Amazon's Alexa tapped Anthropic's Claude . Apple's already shelling out billions to run its own models in the cloud starting next year .  The stock market is down more than 17% year to date, and close to 18% in the past six months alone . It’s down 17% this year, and 18% since the start of the year . Sirifirst appeared on TheStreet on Jul 1, 2025 .

Artificial intelligence continues to make its presence felt in just about everything humans do . The Covid-19 pandemic was a key factor in the 2020-2023 global chip shortage . A 13% increase in global demand for PCs sparked by a sudden shift to working from home added to demand .  September 2020 imposed restrictions on, Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corp., which made it harder for China's largest chip maker to sell to companies with American ties . But by January 2023, Peter Voser, chairman of the chairman of Swedish-Swiss tech and engineering giant ABBtold CNBCthat the global semiconductor shortage was “being sorted out”  Onshoring and reshoring involves bringing production back to a company’s home country, while nearshoring relocates operations to a nearby country . The chip industry has flipped from growth to shrinkage nine times in the last 34 years .  Bernstein raised the firm's price target on Amazon on July 1 to $235 from $230 . The firm noted that the growth of Amazon Web Services (AWS), the company's cloud computing platform, decelerated sequentially in Q1 to +17% year-over-year .  Amazon's contribution continues to grow triple digits year-over-year from a relatively smaller base compared with Microsoft's cloud-computing platform . Amazon remains supply constrained on chips, motherboards and other components, which has put a near-term cap on topline growth . In this episode of ETF Spotlight, I speak with Tim Rotolo, Founder and CEO of Range Fund Holdings, about investing in the nuclear renaissance . President Trump’s recent executive orders on nuclear power outline a sweeping agenda to quadruple U.S. nuclear capacity by 2050 . The renewed interest in nuclear energy is largely fueled by Big Tech's push into the sector, as AI data centers require massive amounts of energy .  Building nuclear power plants is notoriously expensive and time-consuming . SMRs are typically manufactured in factories and can reduce construction time and costs . Nuclear fusion is often called the "holy grail" of energy because it promises a virtually limitless, clean, and safe source of power by replicating the process that fuels the stars .
